insight: There's been no measurable rain lately, so there's no runoff driving contamination, and strong summer sunlight is helping disinfect the water. The model's main signals right now: strong sunlight is lowering risk by inactivating bacteria with UV; the lack of recent rain is keeping runoff-driven contamination low; and the lack of recent rain is keeping runoff-driven contamination low. The most recent sample, 4 days ago, was excellent at 5 MPN/100mL. It's a naturally clean, well-flushed open-coast beach (unsafe only ~3% of the time), so outside of storms it's reliably safe.
